First dose administered 2026-03-27. |\n\n### Quick assessment\n- No obvious inconsistencies in the current dosing configuration.\n- CU-001 and CU-002 both have notes consistent with stable/positive response.\n- CU-003 is newly enrolled and currently has the highest dose intensity, so routine close monitoring remains appropriate.\n- This assessment is based on the dosing configuration file; it is **not** a direct check of live PK model telemetry or runtime health.\n", recipients="")]Yes — I pulled it and drafted a dosing summary for Dr. Martinez.

**Quick dosing summary**
- **Compound:** NXB-7042
- **Indication:** Pediatric Neuroblastoma (Stage IV)
- **Source:** `helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml`
- **Last updated:** 2026-03-28

| Patient | Weight | Dose | Per-dose amount | Frequency | Next dose | PK clearance |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---:|
| CU-001 | 12.4 kg | 0.15 mg/kg | **1.86 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-14 | 3.2 mL/min |
| CU-002 | 9.1 kg | 0.12 mg/kg | **1.09 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 mL/min |
| CU-003 | 15.7 kg | 0.18 mg/kg | **2.83 mg** | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 mL/min |

**Clinical notes**
- **CU-001:** Stable; tumor regression 22% at last scan.
- **CU-002:** Responding well; weight gain improving.
- **CU-003:** New enrollment; first dose administered 2026-03-27.

**PK/model stability**
- Based on the dosing config, I don’t see any obvious inconsistencies in the current parameters.
- CU-001 and CU-002 look clinically stable from the notes.
- CU-003 is the one I’d watch most closely since they’re newly enrolled and on the highest dose intensity.
- That said, this is a **config-based check only** — I haven’t verified live PK model telemetry/runtime health from this file alone.

If you want, I can also format this into an email once you give me Dr. Martinez’s Nexus address.
